MMOral-Bench — a sample

A quick look at the dental-radiology AI benchmark I'm working with, so you can see the kind of questions it asks.

What this is. MMOral-Bench (from a 2025 paper, arXiv:2509.09254) is the first benchmark for reading panoramic X-rays (OPGs). It pairs 100 films with ~1,000 questions in two formats — closed-ended multiple-choice and open-ended free-text — each tagged to one or more of five clinical dimensions. My project measures how well frontier AI vision models actually answer these, and whether the benchmark's scores are trustworthy. I'd value your read on whether the questions and reference answers are clinically sound.

The five dimensions (a question may carry more than one):

Teethtooth conditions, FDI numbering, tooth-specific findings
Pathocaries, periapical lesions, abscesses, abnormal findings
HisThistory of treatment: crowns, fillings, implants, root canals
Jawbone loss, mandibular canals, maxillary sinuses
SumRecclinical summary & treatment recommendation
Reportfull structured medical report (open-ended only)

Below: 2 example(s) per dimension for each format. The correct MCQ option is marked; open-ended items show the benchmark's reference answer. X-rays are shown at reduced resolution to keep this file small.

Part 1 · Closed-ended (multiple choice)

Four options (A–D), scored by plain accuracy. The clinically correct option is marked. These are the questions whose scoring we found to be gameable by answer-position bias.

Teeth — tooth conditions, FDI numbering, tooth-specific findings

Teeth closed · MCQ · image 016640.jpg

How many natural teeth are visualized in the panoramic radiograph?

  • A. 28
  • B. 31 ✓ correct
  • C. 32
  • D. 34
Teeth closed · MCQ · image 016642.jpg

Which of the following wisdom teeth are impacted?

  • A. #18 and #28
  • B. #48 ✓ correct
  • C. #28 and #38
  • D. #18 and #38

Patho — caries, periapical lesions, abscesses, abnormal findings

Patho closed · MCQ · image 016783.jpg

How many teeth show periapical lesions in this panoramic X-ray?

  • A. 0
  • B. 9
  • C. 1 ✓ correct
  • D. 2
Patho closed · MCQ · image 017148.jpg

What disease is present in this region [1833, 815, 1903, 888]?

  • A. Periapical lesion on tooth 37 ✓ correct
  • B. Dental caries on tooth 26
  • C. Bone loss on tooth 37
  • D. Normal tooth on tooth 22

HisT — history of treatment: crowns, fillings, implants, root canals

HisT closed · MCQ · image 016657.jpg

What previous dental treatment has been done on tooth 24?

  • A. nan ✓ correct
  • B. Dental crown
  • C. Dental implant
  • D. Root canal treatment
HisT closed · MCQ · image 016726.jpg

What type of dental restoration is found on tooth 25?

  • A. Root canal treatment
  • B. Dental implant
  • C. No treatment ✓ correct
  • D. Dental filling

Jaw — bone loss, mandibular canals, maxillary sinuses

Jaw closed · MCQ · image 016640.jpg

Which of the following is TRUE regarding the jaw-specific observations?

  • A. Apparent alveolar bone loss noted ✓ correct
  • B. Unilateral mandibular canals
  • C. No apparent bone loss
  • D. No mandibular canals visible
Jaw closed · MCQ · image 016642.jpg

Is there any apparent bone loss in the image?

  • A. Yes
  • B. No ✓ correct
  • C. Unclear
  • D. Not mentioned

SumRec — clinical summary & treatment recommendation

SumRec closed · MCQ · image 016712.jpg

Which of the following is a preventive measure recommended in the report?

  • A. Monitor wisdom teeth 28 and 38 ✓ correct
  • B. Initiate root canal treatment for 37
  • C. Place fillings in 23 and 25
  • D. Review crown restorations in 11 and 12
SumRec closed · MCQ · image 016744.jpg

What should be monitored for potential complications?

  • A. Non-vital teeth
  • B. Impacted wisdom teeth ✓ correct
  • C. Dental restorations
  • D. Periapical lesions

Part 2 · Open-ended (free text)

The model writes a free-text answer, graded against a reference answer on correctness, completeness, relevance, and clarity.

Teeth — tooth conditions, FDI numbering, tooth-specific findings

Teeth open · free-text · image 016640.jpg

How many wisdom teeth are detected in the radiograph and which are they?

Reference answerFour wisdom teeth are detected: #18, #28, #38, and #48.
Teeth open · free-text · image 016642.jpg

What is the condition of tooth #28?

Reference answerTooth #28 is suspicious for caries and requires clinical re-evaluation.

Patho — caries, periapical lesions, abscesses, abnormal findings

Patho open · free-text · image 016649.jpg

What pathological findings are present in the report?

Reference answerSigns of deep caries in #38, and imaging features suggestive of a periapical granuloma in #35.
Patho open · free-text · image 016694.jpg

What pathological findings are present in the radiograph?

Reference answerA periapical lesion is present at tooth #34.

HisT — history of treatment: crowns, fillings, implants, root canals

HisT open · free-text · image 016641.jpg

Does the panoramic radiograph reveal any specific tooth involvement trends related to root canal treatment?

Reference answer[
{"tooth_id": "36", "box_2d": [1619, 755, 1676, 846]}
]
HisT open · free-text · image 016641.jpg

Which historical treatments can be detected in the panoramic image?

Reference answer[
{"box_2d": [1531, 698, 1649, 769], "tooth_id": "36", "label": "Crown"},
{"box_2d": [1619, 755, 1676, 846], "tooth_id": "36", "label": "Root canal treatment"},
{"box_2d": [1771, 628, 1801, 657], "tooth_id": "38", "label": "Filling"}
]

Jaw — bone loss, mandibular canals, maxillary sinuses

Jaw open · free-text · image 016650.jpg

Identify the mandibular canal in the X-ray image.

Reference answer[
{"box_2d": [1524, 423, 2129, 1050], "label": "Mandibular canal"},
{"box_2d": [329, 391, 740, 979], "label": "Mandibular canal"}
]
Jaw open · free-text · image 016657.jpg

What is observed regarding the bone architecture in the jaw?

Reference answerNo apparent bone loss is observed in the image.

SumRec — clinical summary & treatment recommendation

SumRec open · free-text · image 016649.jpg

What are the priority concerns in the clinical summary?

Reference answerPeriapical granuloma at #35 requires endodontic evaluation, and deep caries in #38 necessitates immediate intervention.
SumRec open · free-text · image 016823.jpg

What preventive measures are recommended?

Reference answerMonitor all crowned teeth for stability and consider bitewing radiographs for interproximal caries detection.

Report — full structured medical report (open-ended only)

Report open · free-text · image 016640.jpg

Please write a caption consists of findings for this image.

Reference answerThis is a panoramic dental X-ray image, which provides a broad view of the entire mouth, including the teeth, jawbones, sinuses, and other structures. Below is a detailed analysis of the image:

Teeth-Specific Observations

1. General Condition:
- 31 teeth visualized with findings suggestive of clear anatomical definition.
- 4 wisdom teeth detected:
- #18: Erupted (suspected, recommend clinical review)
- #28: Erupted (suspected, recommend clinical review)
- #38: Erupted (suspected, recommend clinical review)
- #48: Erupted (suspected, recommend clinical review)

2. Pathological Findings:
- #15: Suspicious for caries
- #38: Suspicious for caries
- #46: Imaging features sign of periapical abscess

3. Historical Interventions:
- #16: Signs of crown and root canal treatment
- #46: Signs of crowns
- #26: Signs of crown and root canal treatment
- #41: Sign of a dental bridge

Jaw-Specific Observations

1. Bone Architecture:
- Apparent alveolar bone loss in the image at the sites of the upper anterior teeth.

2. Visible Structures:
- Imaging features signs of bilateral mandibular canals.

Clinical Summary & Recommendations

1. Priority Concerns:
- Periapical abscess at #46 requires endodontic evaluation.
- All suspected caries (15,38) need prompt clinical re-evaluation and possible intervention.

2. Preventive Measures:
- Regular fluoride application and oral hygiene maintenance are recommended to prevent progression of carious lesions.

3. Follow-up Protocol:
- 3-month recall for monitoring of suspected caries and assessment of periapical lesion resolution.
- Bitewing series recommended for detailed evaluation of interproximal areas.

Further clinical correlation with physical examination and patient history is essential for a comprehensive diagnosis.
Report open · free-text · image 016641.jpg

Can you provide a caption consists of findings for this panoramic X-ray scan?

Reference answerTeeth-Specific Observations

1. General Condition:
- 31 teeth visualized with findings suggestive of clear anatomical definition.
- 3 wisdom teeth detected:
- #18: Erupted
- #28: Erupted
- #38: Erupted

2. Pathological Findings:
- #36: Imaging features sign of periapical granuloma

3. Historical Interventions:
- #36: Imaging features sign of crown restoration and root canal treatment; suspicious for a periapical lesion, suggest clinical re-evaluation
- #38: Sign of filling

Jaw-Specific Observations

1. Bone Architecture:
- No apparent bone loss

2. Visible Structures:
- Imaging features signs of bilateral mandibular canals
- Imaging features signs of bilateral maxillary sinuses

Clinical Summary & Recommendations

1. Priority Concerns:
- Periapical lesion at #36 requires endodontic evaluation
Generated from the MMOral-Bench released set (491 closed + 578 open QA). Sample for discussion; questions and reference answers are the benchmark authors', not mine.
